over to Kevin. Take it away.

I'm at. If you guys want to ask questions queue them up for everybody and we'll
get into it.

were because the key here as you can see... Am I sharing my screen by any

you start looking at everything and everyone, every movie, every person you

like actually Maslow makes it... That person is going to behave that way pretty
reliably. But the interesting thing about the human organism, that's what
Maslow says a lot here is that we have these different preferences. It's not all
rigid. Only the bottom of the pyramid is very rigid on our behavior. Everything
else is kind of a cocktail of whatever it is that you have going on your life and
what your preferences are. So some things higher up in the pyramid get waited
differently. It's like a very dynamic motivations and that's a very important
thing to know. But there's still these general motivations. Let's start off. Here's
the quote for Maslow. Let me put this in presentation mode. Here it says a
musician must make music, an artist must paint, a poet must write. To be
ultimately happy what a man can be must be. This means we make all self-
actualizations. This is Maslow, and this is his key things.
I'm going into a quote next. This is quoted from a book that we put on a book. I
wasn't going to tell anybody about this book for the longest time. It's actually a
bit of secret for about 2 years, and I kept to the secret even with... The guy we're
having on tomorrow which is David Garfinkel. He's been obsessed with story.

called me back, we talked about a week later and he didn't tell me he went off
and bought the book already. He's like, "Oh my god Kevin, 20 years I've been
looking for the answer to how this all works. This made it all fall into place. I
want to tell you a secret. This thing is the psychological breakdown of how
humans and story are like... That was a good intro for creating a reason why
you need to value this webinar.
I'm going to read you these excerpts. A lot of this webinar is excerpts. And I'm
inspired to hear it from the horse's mouth, and we'll just go and do side
commentary about it. Most of us were bought to believe that reason and
emotion are polar opposites, with reason as Stalwart white hat and in motion
as the silky black cat. Let's not even discuss which gender will set to where
which hat. The reason it was thought sees the world as it is while the rational
scam, emotion, tries to undermine it. It turns out as neuroscience writer Jonah
Lehrer says if it weren't for our emotions reasons wouldn't exist at all. Take
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 24 --

Kevin Halbert on Maslow and Marketing
www.halbertology.com 4
that Plato. This is illustrated by a sad story that even [Unintelligible 00:06:23]
it's a real-life protagonist doesn't see as sad at all because he can't literally.
Eliot, a patient of Antonio Damasio had lost a small section of his pre-frontal
cortices during surgery for a benign brain tumor. Before his illness Elliot held a
high-level corporate job and had a happy, thriving family.
By the time he saw Damasio, Elliot was in the process of losing everything. He
still tested in the 97% of [Unintelligible 00:06:56] IQ, had a high functioning
memory, and had no trouble enumerating each and every possible solution to
a problem. The trouble was he couldn't make decisions from what color pen to
use to whether it was more important to do the work his boss expected or to
spend the day alphabetizing all the folders in his office? Why? Because as
Damasio had discovered, the damage to his brain left him unable to experience
emotion. As a result he was utterly detached and approach life as if everything
in it was neutral. But wait, shouldn't that be a good thing? Now that emotion
couldn't butt in in Clyde Elliot's judgment he'd be free to make rational
decisions, right? I think you know where this is going. Without emotion each
option carried the exact same weight. Everything really was 6 of 1, half a dozen
of the other.
So it turns out a cognitive scientist Steven Pinker notes emotions are
mechanisms that set the brain's highest level goals, along with apparently every
other goal, down to what to have for breakfast. Without emotions Elliot had no
way to gauge what was important and what wasn't, what mattered and what
didn't is exactly the same when it comes to story. If the reader can't feel what
matters and doesn't then nothing matters, including finishing the story. The
question for writers then is where do these feelings come from. The answer is
very simple, the protagonist.
This is why all these feelings and emotions, we're going to get into it more.
We're moving on to Maslow and this is his hierarchy. The more we're at the
bottom here the more we have a predictable behavior, the more we pretty
much don't have a choice for what we prioritize. And as we go up it becomes
more and more optional so to speak. These are kind of loose categories. You see
air, water, food, shelter, sleep, and clothing. They all kind of in a similar race but
they all trump, or what he calls a propotent ownership over the person's
behavior before they can move on to this one. Basically, there's no 100% that
when you go from one to the other you have to have at least a majority of the
needs in each of the categories taken care of before the average person will be
able to move up to this level. They have to have an average of this taking care
of before they move up to next. And so on...

Security is the one thing some people are looking for and trying to address
most. Security can be described as more than but giving you the physical,
mental, or whatever. The eminence of death or for whatever reason also may
but now suddenly put us in a state of threat for that for the reason that we may
lose our basic self-confidence here, when we can no longer handle the situation
when the world is too much for us, when we are not masters of our own fate,
when we no longer have control over the world or ourselves, certainly we may
speak of feelings of threat. Other situations in which there is nothing we do
about it are also sometimes felt to be threatening. Perhaps severe pain should
be added into this category. This is certainly something that we can do nothing
about. Suffice to say that the description above would easily describe shell
shock, or someone experiencing a bank robbery. The important point here is
not to take your reader or viewing audience if you're doing VSL's out of their
comfort zone. It's a very important balance to strike when you are talking to
your audience. We'll get into it later but sometimes a person is already in there
on comfort zones. Sometimes you have to pull them back into their own
comfort zone to basically sell the comfort. You have to remind them of the pain
or what's missing in their lives.
The only reason I'm going to do these lists here and they're being list out one
at a time is these are great themes. These are great stories, great things to focus
on, and great tones to take when you're writing a copy. Insecurity, feeling of
rejection of being unloved, being treated coldly, without affection, or being
hated, or being despised all very strong motivators. Feelings of isolation,
ostracism, aloneness, being out of it, feelings of perceptional world and life as
dangerous, threatening, dark, hostile, or challenging. As a general rule in which
every man's hand is against every others, in which one eats or is eaten are
section of other human beings as essentially bad, evil, or selfish, as dangerous,
threatening, hostile, or challenging. Constant feelings of threat and danger
anxiety. Feelings of suspicion and mistrust of envy or jealousy towards others,
much hostility, prejudices, and hatred. The tendency to expect the worst,
general pessimism. Tendency to be unhappy or discontented. Feelings of
tension, strain, and conflict together with various consequences of tension
which nervousness, fatigue, irritability, nervous stomach, and other
psychosomatic disturbances, nightmares, emotional instability, vacillation,
uncertainty, and inconsistency. Tendency to compulsive introspectiveness,
morbid self-examination, acute consciousness of self. Guilt and shamed
feelings, same feelings. Feeling of self-condemnation, suicidal tendencies,
discouragement, disturbances of various aspects of the self-esteem complex.
Continuous striving for and hunger for safety and security, various neurotic

The insecure person then perceives the world as a threatening jungle and most
human beings as dangerous and selfish, feels rejected and isolated, anxious and
hostile, is generally pessimistic and unhappy. Just signs of tension and conflict
tends to turn inward as trouble by guilt feelings has wondering other
disturbance of self-esteem tends to be or actually is neurotic and is generally

being liked or loved. This is all the after stuff. This is what your product will do
for people when they get to the other side. Feeling of being liked or loved,
acceptance of being looked upon with warmth, feelings of belonging, of being
at home in the world, and having a place in the group, and perception of life as
pleasant, warm, friendly, or benevolent in which all men tend to be brothers.

or benevolent. Feelings of safety, feelings of threat and danger on the
anxiousness. Feelings of friendliness and trust in others, a little hostility
towards others, easy affection for others. The tendency to expect good to
happen, general optimism just need to be happy or contented. Feelings of calm,
ease, and relaxation, unconflicted emotion stability. Tendency to outgoingness.

strength or adequacy with respect to problems rather than her power over
other people. Firm, positive, well-based self-esteem, feeling of strength,
courage. Relative lack of neurotic or psychotic tendencies is really strong.

pull them out of their comfort zone and you say, "You're not sleeping well at
night. I bet you're staying up because of this or because of that." It's more like I
was staying up at night. I was not sleeping well. This wasn't going well in my
life." And then this happened, and then you turn it to all the positive. Usually
that's the part where we'll start bringing the positive onto the reader. We'll say,
"I was sleeping in my car. I was down and out. I was feeling frustrated. It felt
like they didn't belong in the world." And then I made the breakthrough, the
revolution. I discovered the Gary Halbert Letter or whatever. And then you go
through the benefits. I just wanted to point that out.

Kevin: For brevity I want to cut them all off but it's so damn good I can't leave any of
them out. The only thing I want to say here now that we're reminding you is
one of the things that you do, and you go through a painful story. Like with Gary
Halbert you'll go the amazing diet secret, the desperate housewife from Ohio.
The thing is, is that tells you that you're going to hear a painful story. But it also
tells you you're going to have a happy ending. And that's a big difference for a
person that knows it's going to get better. Because if you're drawing the pain
and someone else has exactly like yours, they'll keep breeding because they're
drawn into it. But if they feel there's no hope at the end there's no reason to
read that. That's just making their life miserable. Keep in mind when you do
something like that, that if you're going to take the parallel path with the reader,
that they know that there's light at the end of the tunnel and it's not a train.

Kevin: Okay. Just remember the wire for story readings, it's all connected. All these
things chase each other. In every insecure person with whom the writer has
worked he is also found a continual, never dying longing for security.
Sometimes this has been conscious, sometimes unconscious, and unrecognized
by the subject. Furthermore, they were found wide variations in the subject’s
individual definitions of security that they long for. Some defined it in terms
merely of safety, some in terms of dependent upon a stronger person, some in
terms of how in power and money. And a few define it in what is a more correct
way, i.e. a longing to deserve and win back the love and affection of other
people. Some miscellaneous are these various longings that if we were to be
really conservative we should say only that there was a response of
dissatisfaction and a longing for a more satisfactory state. This is more
satisfactory state being defined individually, how and is very distinguished at
least three levels of satisfactoriness. We're getting up to those.
One, safety, two, the longingness, and three, receiving love and affection, all
very powerful motivators. Almost continual action toward you're gaining this
individually defined security was found in all cases. A lot of times we know that
it could take something away, they'll fight more to get it back if they had it to
begin with. So however this long for saying it was the individual almost always
did something to try to achieve it. Discouragement reactions maybe a response
to failure of these attempts, and to further threat the security to introspective
moods. To invidious comparisons with others, or anything else that drives
home the realization of rejection, being hated, loss of love, etc. These
discouragement reactions will be found frequently if one looks deeply...

why... This is where you address previous failures of your prospect's attempts
to achieve security, to reinstill hope. Describe their failure and details so that

Revenge reactions for love already lost or for threatened lost. Here we may
speak of the general aggressiveness and hostility of the a secure person, of his
bitterness, his antagonism is tendency to hate everyone. If that reaction present
further loss of like, the realization that certain kind of behavior leads to
rejection may lead to inhibition of this behavior or even reaction formations,
e.g., the person realizing consciously or unconsciously that stinginess will be
disapproved by other people may not stop being stingy but may become
excessively generous or extravagant. We may include here also the various
white or avoidance reaction which usually are largely defenses. Okay, trying to
get on this as fast as possible.
Ameliorative reactions to make the best situation more bearable... Here we may
have the sour grace mechanism in which there may be conscious attempts to
save oneself from hurt by changing attitudes toward that which was desired.
Here an individual may say after all there are many advantages in not having
too many friends, or not being married. Thus he may attempt to make the best
of the bad situation. Again, this is objection raising that you guys might want to
bring up depending on what you have to sell.
Attack reactions upon the situations which bring about the insecurity in an
attempt to change or improve them. This step may be little [Unintelligible
00:24:23], for example physical attack upon the person or maybe general social
radicalism to change the factors inside that bring about insecurity such as
unemployment, exploitation, etc. The attack reaction may be good, that is a
realistic one but it may also be a blame reaction, a protective one in which the
individual tests to save his tattered self-esteem by blaming others completely
when he's himself is also to blame. Blame reaction maybe paralyzing since they
tend to make it impossible to invite the real security by making it more difficult
for the person to analyze the situation so it may have improved out years... The
thing is that a person that is so entrenched in this is probably going to quickly
embrace somebody who says the same thing, that misery loves company. If you
find a guy that blames the whole world for his situation on this factor, this
person, this set of people... you start speaking the same way. You got a person
that's ready to bond with you. You have a friend, a brother in arms so to speak.

separable variables tend to go away with each other. Any attack on security
tends to also be an attack on self-esteem, very much so, very true. That goes

someone behaving unselfishly he will tend to manufacture or project selfish
motives upon this unselfish person. What does this mean for the understanding
of the insecure individual? As he becomes more secure in one department of
life, in spirit the department of life being in one area he may expect the
automatic tendency towards insecurity and all departments of life in his
thinking, his perceptions, his remembering, his forgetting, his emotions, his
philosophy. This is also true in the type of areas of security, [Unintelligible
00:29:47] elements. A person who becomes insecure in his relation to his
employers tends to also and certainly become an insecure all social areas in the
sexual life, in his relations, to his family, etc.
The effects of all these reactions on the other people so further to perpetuate
and reinforce the insecurity. [Unintelligible 00:30:13] has described this well
in the concept of the vicious circle. Thus if the insecure person is mistrustful his
mistrust changes his behavior, willy-nilly in such a way so that other people
will be mistrustful of him. This in turn intensifies his mistrust, which in turn
intensifies their mistrust.
And so the person who for whatever reason hates other people will make them
hate him, which will make him hate them even more, etc. Contrary wise, the
person who loves other people is loved more by them for easy discernible
reason. We've seen this with John Riley was on the show. He's a total optimist.
This confirms and strengthens his love for other people which confirms their
love for him.
Limiting the basis the security for safety. Frequently enough we observe that
what's seems at first to be a paradox in front of security, is that what we value
description of the world class negros of the southern towns and overprotected
children in gold [Unintelligible 00:31:23] see the grain injured cases, these are
people... This is written in the 40's, just so you guys know. These are people
who maintain a parent’s security simply by narrating the effective world in
which they live. This can be done by completely giving one's autonomy out to
someone else. The slave or the overprotected child. By lowering the level of
aspiration very markedly we're living only in that area of like which is
absolutely familiar to save or predict growth. Is this way a kind of safety or
pseudo safety can be attained which we shall call security on a narrow base. I'm
going to get into an example. So long as there's someone else to solve all
problems for the end of those work, so long as he stays within his restricted
world, or so long as he asks for nothing and hopes for nothing just so long he
will obtain his apparent security. The security obviously depends entirely on a
continuation of the necessary conditions. With these necessary conditions are

Bond: One of the first case to really using Maslow's hierarchy it means that you can
do it is to actually go ahead and understand it, and admit the things that are
going on with yourself. One of the fundamental, really key tools that any
marketer has is the ability to step into the shoes of the prospect and understand
what they're thinking and what they're saying. I want to give you an example
of what Kevin was talking about with their needs and their hidden needs. And
their acceptance in groups and stuff like that and I'm going to use a watch for
it. You're one of the people who buy high-end watches and the real underlying
reason that nobody wants to admit is because they want to brag. They want to
show off their watches, it's a big piece of jewelry. What they will give the front

excuses that they can tell their friends that are based on keeping them happy in
their groups and not standing... Why are you not going to look like a fool when
you got this. And you do that with the stories, and you do with the stories of
what you're doing with it. But you give them their... This is made with 400 man-
hours are put in to effort the production of every watch and so forth. And that's
all the PC stuff and the reasoning that they can go around and share and be
comfortable that they're not going to be pulled out of their comfort zone by
buying a watch that everybody's going to say, "Hey, why did you waste so much
money on a risk clock as they call it? When you want to want into this
underlining things which you don't want to admit, because you'll be able to
brag, and show it off, and rub your outfit to everybody's face. They don't want
to say that. But what you can do is you can tell those subliminally in the story.
And then actually after I got it I started noticing I would get seated at a better
spot in the restaurant. All of a sudden it was little easier for people to take me
seriously. These are only acceptance and those group things that they want to
be elevated to. And the core emotions mean and you don't want to admit and
they don't want to admit. And that's how you cave in.

So, the insecure man who is highly attractive to women had to see the world in
such a way that he could continue believing that no one loved him. When it was
pointed out to him that women were obviously in love in him. And when the
attempt was made to prove this to him by their actions. He disagreed. And it

women look down upon me so much that everyone who sees me starts being
maternal to me and pities me. It is only facts as we perceived that we can deal

individual we come back to our starting point, a personality individual who
does the perceiving on how this personality came to be. Lovely stuff.
If we say that John Smith is an insecure individual, what does this mean? It
means that he will proceed insecurely, just as he moans insecurely, as he thinks
insecurely, remembers insecurely, philosophizes insecurely, and does
everything else insecure. In short, we will perceive not only what there is to
perceive but what he has learned to perceive when he's prepared or ready to
perceive, or what the logic of his world outlook demands that it perceive at the
same time he will tend to be blind to what he must not or dare not see.

Our first preposition states that an individual is an integrated, organized whole.
The theoretical statement is usually accepted piously enough by psychologist
who then often perceive commonly to more in their actual experiments. That is
an experimental reality as well as a theoretical one must be realized before
sound experimentation and sound motivation theory are possible. In
motivation theory this proposition means many specific things. For instance it
means the whole individual is motivated rather than just part of him. A good
theory there is no such entity as in need of the stomach or mouth, or a generally
need. There's only a need of the individual. It is John Smith who wants food not
John Smith's stomach. Furthermore satisfaction comes from to the whole
individual and not just a part of him. Food satisfies John Smith's hunger and not
his stomach's hunger. That's an important thing to know. The stomach sends
us a hunger signal to your brain. It's your brain. Your brain actually has a copy
of your whole entire map of your body inside of it. And that's the part that
suffers when you're hungry. Something's trying to mess with my webcam. You
guys didn't lose me, right? Okay good.

Kevin: Okay. You guys didn't need to look at me, you need to look at screen edits. Pay
attention kids. Dealing with John Smith's hunger merely of his gastrointestinal
track has made experimenters neglect the fact that when an individual's hungry
he changes not only his gastrointestinal functions but in many, perhaps even
most other functions of which he's capable. His perception's changed. He will
perceive food more readily than he will at other times. His memories change.
He's more after remember a good meal at this time than at other times. His
emotion's changed. He's more tense and nervous than he is at other times. The
content of his thinking changes, he is more have to think of getting food than
solving an algebraic problem. And this list can be extended to almost every
other faculty, capacity or function both physiological and psychic. In other

are usually means to an end rather than ends in themselves, if it's the money.
We want money so that we may have an automobile. This is features and
benefits that will be coming up in the future with us. In turn we want an
automobile because the neighbors have one. You want a watch, right? And we
wish not to feel unfair to them so that we can retain our own self-respect, so
that we can be loved and respected by others. Usually when a conscious desire
is analyzed we find that we can go behind it so to speak to other, more
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 24 --

Kevin Halbert on Maslow and Marketing
www.halbertology.com 17
fundamental aims of the individual. In other words we have here the situation
which parallels very much the role of symptoms in psychopathology.
Symptoms are important not so much in themselves but for what they
ultimately mean, that is for what their ultimate growth or effects are for what
they're trying to do, or for what they're functions may be. The study of
symptoms in themselves is quite unimportant. But the study of the dynamic
meaning of symptoms is important because it is fruitful. For instance making
possible psychotherapy. In particular, desires which pass through our
consciousness dozens of times a day are not in themselves so important as what
they stand for, what they lead to, what they ultimately mean upon deeper
analysis.
There's that anthropological evidence to indicate that the ultimate desires of all
human beings do not differ nearly as much as do their conscious every day
desires or undercurrents are all the same. The main reason for this is that two
different cultures may provide two completely different ways to satisfy a
particular desire, let's say for self-esteem and what society will obtain self-
esteem by being a good hunter and another society of being a great medicine
man, or a bold warrior, or a very unemotional person, and so on. It may be that
if we think of ultimate’s, the one individual's desire to be a good hunter has the
same dynamics and the same fundamental aim as the desire of the other
individual to be a good medicine man. We may then assert that it would be more
useful for psychologists to combine these two seemingly [Unintelligible
00:50:30] conscious desires into the same category rather than to put them into
different categories purely on behavioral grounds. Apparently ends in
themselves are far more universal than the roads taken to achieve those ends,
where these roads are determined locally in the specific culture.
People always come to Gary and say, "My customers are different." And he'd be
like, "No, they're not. My customers are sophisticated. My customers are
educated. My customers are..." No, it all goes down to the same stuff. Man is one-
teamed animal and rarely reaches a state of complete satisfaction except for a
short time. I hope that sinks in there. That means there's always something they
can be paying off and we just have to find the people where they are in the
pitfall. Our audience is in that part of the pyramid. As one desire is satisfied,
another one pops out to take its place. When this is satisfied still another comes
into the foreground, etc. It is a characteristic of the human being throughout his
whole life that he's practically always desiring something. I will go to Buddha
but he thinks all suffering comes from wanting or desire every day, which I
think all suffering comes from trying to avoid it.

third, which is prepotent over the fourth, which is prepotent over the fifth. This

of our population. This prepotency means primarily that, where all the
warrants are unsatisfied but one that will attempt to dominate consciousness
and behavior will be the search for conscious and behavior will be the search
for gratification. It is often found for the person with all the warrants

makes a naive assumption that if he is satisfied with these wants he will then
be happy and contented, but this is not so. But the most dominant ones is
satisfied a new one emerges into consciousness. Usually the next higher in the
hierarchy of prepotency. If this second want is satisfied then a third want
appears from nowhere. Ordinarily the satisfaction of these ones are not
altogether initially exclusive or so early as a matter of degree. The average

Okay, moving on, back to Maslow. Physical needs, homeostasis refers to the
body's automatic efforts to maintain a constant normal state of bloodstream.
Kennedy has described this process for one, water content of the blood, two
salt content, three sugar content, four protein content, five fat content, six
calcium content, seven oxygen content, eight, constant hydrogen ion level, acid-
base balance or pH, and nine constant temperature of the blood. Obviously, this
list can be extended to include other minerals, the hormones of pregnancy, etc.

relation to body needs. If the body lacks some chemical the individual tend to
develop a specific appetite of partial hunger for that food element. I find that

other, of other motivations, and as the organism as a whole. And secondly, in

If all the needs are unsatisfied the organism is then dominated by the
physiological needs, all of their needs may become simply non-existent having
pushed in the background. It is then characterized the whole organism by
saying simply that it is hungry for consciousness almost completely pre-
empted by hunger. All capacities are put into the service of hunger satisfaction,
and the organization of these capacities almost entirely determined by the one
purpose of satisfying hunger. We're going to get it as level, the drive is kind of
the same for more [Unintelligible 01:03:06]. The intelligence, memory habits,
all we define as simply as hunger gratifying tools, capacities that are not useful
for this purpose lie dormant or pushed in the background. The urge to write
poetry, the desire to acquire an automobile, the interest in American history,
the desire for a new pair of shoes, all in the extreme case forgotten or become
secondary of importance. The matters extremely dangerous and have no other
interest exists but food. He dreams food, he remembers food, he thinks about
food, he knows only about food, he perceives only food, and he wants only food.
Some of the terms that ordinarily fuse with the physiological drives and
organizing, even feeding, drinking, or sexual [Unintelligible 01:03:53] are now
being may be so completely overrun as to allow us to speak at this but only at
this time a pure hunger driving behavior with the one unqualified aim of relief.
No particular characters of human organism when it's dominated by a certain
need is that the whole philosophy of the future tends to also change, for a
currently and extremely hungry man Utopia could be defined very simply as a
place where there is plenty of food. Think of an oasis in the desert. He tends to
think that. If only he's guaranteed food for the rest of his life he will be perfectly
happy and will never want for anything more. Life sends in itself tends to be
defined in terms of eating. Anything else will be found as unimportant.
Freedom, wealth, community, feeling, respect, philosophy may all be way
decide as [Unintelligible 01:04:50] which are useless since they failed to fill the
stomach. Such a man may fairly be said to live by [Unintelligible 01:04:56]. Put
them up, I want you guys too much. This is going to get important.

important a concept as deprivation and motivation theory for it releases an

performance is not indicative of future results" really holds true here.
One of the limitations of hypothetical performance results is that they are generally prepared with the benefit
of hindsight. Also, hypothetical trading does not involve financial risk and that’s why no hypothetical profit
record can completely account for the impact of financial risk in business.
There are a lot of other factors related to the market in general or to the implementation of any specific
business objective which cannot be fully accounted for in the preparation of hypothetical performance results
and all of which can adversely affect results.
Although we make every attempt to assure accuracy, we do not give any express or implied warranty as to its
accuracy. What we are saying here is that we do not accept any liability for any error or omission. You'll see
we have examples posted now and again. These are for illustrative purposes only so please do not be
construe them as investment advice or strategy.
Halbertology (dot) com and (dot) net, Mark McRae, Kevin Halbert and Bond Halbert, the website and its
representatives including product developers and authors and guest speakers do not and cannot give
investment advice or invite customers to engage in investments through this website.
It follows then also that we and anybody else who helped us create, produce, or deliver the site and any
materials bought by you are not liable for any damages you suffer if and when and however you use it/them.
We or any of these persons mentioned above, are never to be held liable by you for any: "direct, incidental,
consequential, indirect, or punitive damages arising out of your access to, or use of, the site or services. or
product or inability to do so either in whole or in part. Without limiting the foregoing, everything on the site
and via services is provided to you 'AS IS' WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EITHER EXPRESSED OR
IMPLIED, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS
FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR NON INFRINGEMENT."

accept this disclaimer agreement as outlined and read with our terms of use and privacy policy.
